ch15_start.text: Amber launch lights strobe the Escape Bay in a rhythm like a slow alarm clock for the end of the world. Gideon Stroud stands at the console with a pneumatic rivet gun held not like a weapon but like a tool that has been waiting for this job. Behind you the blast doors buckle. The organism is almost through — a wet thunder of recognition. Gideon's eyes find yours. He does not look surprised to see you. He looks inconvenienced by a variable.

ch15_confront_gideon.text: 'You're late to a conversation that finished without you,' Gideon says. The rivet gun does not waver. 'The scuttling sequence cannot be stopped from here. If the Toxin reaches the surface, it rewrites the biosphere in days. I am burying it. I am saving Earth. Including the parts of it that signed our death warrant.'
ch15_confront_gideon.speaker: Gideon

choices.text: Present the scuttling auth log — prove you know he chose this.
Present his family note / unsent draft — you know his shame too.
Let the infection answer for you.

ch15_gideon_proof.text: You hold up the auth log. He nods once — almost respectful. 'Yes. My hand. My biometrics. Not a glitch, not a hero AI, not Miller's ghost. Me.' No denial. Denial was always for people who needed to be liked. 'I told you preparedness is not guilt. Sometimes it is both.'
ch15_gideon_proof.speaker: Gideon

choices.text: Demand the rest of the math.

ch15_gideon_shame.text: For the first time something human moves behind his eyes — not weakness. Cost. 'They are on the surface. Eight billion strangers and three people with my name in their mouths. I will not gamble them on a bathysphere that might carry a spore under a fingernail.' He steadies. 'Do not confuse confession with surrender.'
ch15_gideon_shame.speaker: Gideon

ch15_gideon_logic.text: 'Simple math. A hundred lives here for billions above. One seat in the pod. I am taking it — not as reward. As quarantine of last resort: if I go, I go knowing what I carry, and I will not.' The doors scream inward. Rivets tick in the gun like a countdown. What you do next is not a menu. It is the person the depth has made of you.
ch15_gideon_logic.speaker: Gideon

textVariants.text: 'Simple math,' Gideon says, but his voice doubles — one human, one wet echo under your ribs. 'One seat.' The organism offers a third option that wears your hands. The doors scream. Choose while you still can.

choices.text: Accept his logic. Stay. Let the abyss close.
Fight him for the pod — refuse to die for their ledger.
If you assembled the chain — offer a third path: evidence rises with you.
Stop resisting. Open yourself to the deep.

ch15_resolve_fight.text: You move. Rivets spark off the deck plating. The pod hatch yawns like a mouth that has been waiting for any honest answer. Everything you gathered — allies, logs, the weight in your blood, the names you refused to flatten into one villain — decides what kind of survivor climbs out of the dark.

ch15_ending_abyssal_grave.text: You lower your hands. If you rise, you may carry the end of the world in a cut you cannot feel yet. Gideon searches your face for mockery and finds none. He seals the hatch. You sit on cold metal as the blast doors give way and the dark water takes Nereus like a held breath finally released. In the last red light you think of Miller's beach photo, Maya's boots, Aris's shaking hands — a station of people who failed in human ways. Gideon failed in a way that almost looks like love. The abyss does not grade on a curve.

ch15_ending_surface_tension.text: You refuse the dark. The fight is ugly and short and paid for with someone else's calm. You take the pod through violence and luck while Gideon falls into the mathematics he worshiped. As you rise, Nereus folds in on itself below — a secret the ocean keeps, a guilt with your pulse. You survived. The cost is a roster of names you will say wrong for the rest of your life.

ch15_ending_whistleblower.text: You take the pod — not alone in spirit. Evidence rides with you: scuttling biometrics, Corporate's one-line murder, the chain of human failures that made a monster inevitable. When you breach sunlight that feels like a rumor, you broadcast before they can buy your silence. The ocean keeps its dead. The world will not keep this secret. Somewhere, a child on a pier will grow up never knowing a stranger in the dark chose numbers that included her name.

ch15_ending_patient_zero.text: Black veins bloom into scripture. Voices become choir — Miller, the drill team, a kindness that is not kindness. Your jaw unhinges on a gift of spores that blinds Gideon mid-prayer to his math. You take the pod not to live, but to share. The surface has no idea it is about to learn a new language of flesh. In the last human corner of your mind, you almost thank him for keeping the seat warm.

game_over.text: Thank you for playing Abyssal Toxin. Progress remains on this device if you wish to walk another path — dig deeper, accuse better, or fail more honestly. The station is gone. The questions are not.
